2010-03-01.abstract: A police statement gives new details about a January assassination that has been widely attributed to Israel’s intelligence service, though Israel has not acknowledged involvement.

The report said the children numbered in the tens of thousands, not the “hundreds of thousands.”

2010-03-27.title: Catholic Order Admits Its Founder Abused Boys Over Decades
2010-03-27.abstract: A powerful religious order, the Legionaries of Christ,  expressed “sorrow and grief” to anyone “damaged by our founder’s actions.”

2010-03-27.title: Vettel Sets Stage for Australian Grand Prix
2010-03-27.abstract: Sebastian Vettel set the pole position in Australia for Sunday’s Grand Prix.

2010-03-28.title: Quake Accentuated Chasm That Has Defined Haiti
2010-03-28.abstract: The earthquake has focused attention on Haiti’s glaring gap between tiny pockets of wealth and extreme poverty.

2010-03-28.title: South Korea Searches for Survivors of Sunken Navy Ship
2010-03-28.abstract: Hopes were dimming for 46 missing sailors, and the mystery of what caused their ship to break in half in a blast remained unresolved.

2010-03-28.title: Vasily Smyslov, Former World Chess Champion, Dies at 89
2010-03-28.abstract: Mr. Smyslov, who held the championship for just a year, was known for both the artistry of his play and the solidity of his technique.

2010-03-28.title: Kirkuk: Arabs, Baathists, Insurgents and Turkmens United Against the Kurds
2010-03-28.abstract: In Kirkuk Sunni Arabs, including Baathists and former insurgents, came out to vote for Mr. Allawi’s slate. They were joined by Sunni and Shiite Turkmens.

2010-03-28.title: Button Wins Wild Australian Grand Prix
2010-03-28.abstract: Jenson Button won a breathtaking Australian Grand Prix, to take the eighth victory of his career.

2010-03-29.title: Accused in a Ponzi Scheme, Fugitive Extradited to Serbia
2010-03-29.abstract: Jezdimir Vasiljevic faces trial on charges of embezzling more than $38 million and stealing around $130 million from investors in his bank.

2010-03-29.title: Rift Exposes Split in Views on Mideast
2010-03-29.abstract: The U.S. and Israel  differ substantially on the international importance of the Israeli-Palestinian dispute, and on the Palestinians’ readiness for statehood.

2010-03-29.title: Dreaming of Cuban Profits in Post-Embargo World
2010-03-29.abstract: Although Cuba remains closed to American investment, entrepreneurs in both countries are considering the island’s profit-making potential.

2010-03-29.title: North Koreans Use Cellphones to Bare Secrets
2010-03-29.abstract: Citizens feeding information about life in North Korea to the outside world represents a threat to the gulag state.

2010-03-29.title: Obama Team Is Divided on Anti-Terror Tactics
2010-03-29.abstract: Senior lawyers are divided over  the powers, including detention without trial, inherited from former President George W. Bush.

2010-03-30.title: Iraqi Ex-Premier Looks to Past in Fighting Critics
2010-03-30.abstract: Ayad Allawi is determined to counter charges by enemies that he won an election by appealing to Baathists.

2010-03-30.title: A Guiding Voice Amid the Ruins of a Capital City
2010-03-30.abstract: Radio Mogadishu is the only station in south central Somalia where journalists broadcast freely — without fear of being beheaded.

2010-03-30.title: Berlusconi’s Coalition Makes Gains in Italy Regional Vote
2010-03-30.abstract: The vote turned into a referendum of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi’s conservative style, and his coalition managed to wrest four regions from the opposition.

2010-03-30.title: Afghan Leader Is Seen to Flout Influence of U.S.
2010-03-30.abstract: The Obama administration is struggling with how to influence an ally they regard as unreliable.

2010-03-30.title: Blasts Could Derail Medvedev’s Softer Tack in the Caucasus
2010-03-30.abstract: Barely a day after bombs ripped through Moscow subway stations, President Dmitri A. Medvedev discussed poverty in the North Caucasus, a change from the usual Russian rhetoric after attacks.

2010-03-31.title: Pakistan Arrests a Top Crime Official
2010-03-31.abstract: The nation’s Supreme Court ordered the detention to press the government to seek the reopening of corruption cases in Switzerland against President Asif Ali Zardari.

2010-03-31.title: Suicides, Some for Separatist Cause, Jolt India
2010-03-31.abstract: Suicide has become a phenomenon in India, alarming public health experts.

2010-03-31.title: Despite Doubt, Karzai Brother Retains Power
2010-03-31.abstract: Officials have decided that Ahmed Wali Karzai, suspected of ties to drugs and insurgents,  will be allowed to stay.

2010-03-31.title: Journalists’ E-Mails Hacked in China
2010-03-31.abstract: Infiltrations that appeared to be aimed at people who write about China and Taiwan rendered their Yahoo accounts inaccessible, according to those affected.

2010-03-31.title: Haiti Frets Over Aid and Control of Rebuilding
2010-03-31.abstract: President René Préval will present a reconstruction plan to a donors at the United Nations on Wednesday.
